Container.Name: binhex-caddy-duckdns
Container.Overview: Caddy is a powerful, enterprise-ready, open source web server with automatic HTTPS written in Go. This image includes the DuckDNS DNS challenge plugin, enabling automatic TLS certificate issuance and renewal via Let's Encrypt for DuckDNS hostnames without requiring port 80 to be open. Ideal for use as a reverse proxy on a home network.
Container.DonateText: If you appreciate my work, then please consider buying me a beer :D

Config.Name: Port: HTTPS
Config.Description: The HTTPS port Caddy listens on. Must match https_port in your Caddyfile.

Config.Name: Port: HTTP
Config.Description: The HTTP port Caddy listens on (used for redirects). Must match http_port in your Caddyfile.

Config.Name: Port: Admin
Config.Description: The admin edpoint port for Caddy.

Config.Name: Path: /config
Config.Description: This is the container path to your Caddy configuration file (Caddyfile) and logs.

Config.Name: Variable: DUCKDNS_TOKEN
Config.Description: Duck DNS token for automatic TLS certificate issuance and renewal, sign into Duck DNS to view your token.

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_COMMAND
Config.Description: The command or script to execute, if not specified then the script healthcheck.sh will be used (process, dns and https checking).

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_ACTION
Config.Description: The action to execute if the healthcheck command returns a non zero exit code, if not specified the action will be 'exit 1', if you want the container to exit on failure then set the action to 'kill 1'.

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_HOSTNAME
Config.Description: The hostname used when performing HTTPS and DNS healthchecking.

Config.Name: Variable: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for the running container

Config.Name: Variable: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for the running container
